openQuestions.question: Are schemas, handlers, docs, and catalog/playbook maintained from divergent sources of truth, and is there a generator or contract that would close most doc/schema-to-code drift?
openQuestions.answer: The common cause is divergent source-of-truth maintenance across public tool schemas, Zod schemas, handler forwarding, operator docs, and catalog/playbook surfaces. A generator or round-trip test boundary across those contracts would close the largest class.

openQuestions.question: Is metadata drift a generate-context / graph-metadata-backfill systemic defect or mostly isolated edits, and how many packets appear affected?
openQuestions.answer: Metadata drift is systemic. The graph metadata derivation reads frontmatter status and then falls back to implementation-summary/checklist completion, while current specs expose status in markdown metadata tables. A read-only 026 scan found 163 strong status contradictions.

openQuestions.question: Do entity-density cache staleness and atomic-save ordering corrupt retrieval or graph-channel routing under normal single-user operation?
openQuestions.answer: Entity-density staleness is a transient graph/degree routing correctness bug for memory_update, not durable data corruption. Atomic-save ordering is a durable consistency risk because DB indexing can commit before pending-file promotion.

openQuestions.question: Under the local single-user MCP threat model, should the two P0 scope/auth findings remain P0 or be recalibrated?
openQuestions.answer: Recalibrate to conditional P0: P0 for governed or multi-tenant isolation claims, P1 under the default trusted local stdio single-user model.

openQuestions.question: Did deep-loop fan-out reliability defects make prior review or research artifacts suspect?
openQuestions.answer: Prior artifacts are suspect at the orchestration/provenance layer, but not wholly invalid as review content. Use lineage reports and merged findings with caveats; do not trust summaries/attribution as release-gate proof.

resolvedQuestions.answer: Drift is caused by divergent source-of-truth maintenance across schemas, docs, handlers, and catalog/playbook surfaces.
Metadata drift is systemic. Status-source precedence ignores the spec template's visible metadata table and can mark draft/planned specs complete from implementation-summary/checklist evidence.
Entity-density staleness affects query routing freshness; atomic save ordering can create a durable DB/file mismatch on crash or promotion failure.
The scope/auth findings are real for governed scope but should be conditional P0 rather than unconditional P0 under trusted local stdio.
The weak layer is orchestration/provenance, not the existence of per-lineage review content.

resolvedQuestions.question: Common root cause for doc/schema-to-code drift
resolvedQuestions.answer: Divergent hand-maintained contract surfaces: public tool definitions, runtime Zod schemas, allowed-parameter maps, handlers, docs, catalog, and playbook.

resolvedQuestions.question: Metadata-drift systemic-ness
resolvedQuestions.answer: Systemic enough for tooling. Backfill and canonical save refresh metadata, but current checks do not enforce all semantic status, last-active, renumbering, and placeholder invariants.

resolvedQuestions.question: Memory-correctness real impact
resolvedQuestions.answer: Entity-density staleness affects graph-channel routing until invalidation/TTL; atomic save ordering can create an indexed row before final file promotion.

resolvedQuestions.question: P0 security severity calibration
resolvedQuestions.answer: Community fallback is P0 if governed scope is a data boundary; causal bare-ID mutation is P1 under strictly local single-user operation and P0 if tenant/user/agent scope is security isolation.

resolvedQuestions.question: Deep-loop blast radius
resolvedQuestions.answer: Prior fan-out orchestration summaries can mask failed CLI exits and under-delivered concurrency. The eight slice summaries in this audit report one CLI lineage despite five report files per slice.

keyFindings.question: Q1-Q5
keyFindings.title: Eight review slices collapse into six recurring root-cause families
keyFindings.summary: The dominant clusters are MCP contract drift, memory write correctness, governed-scope/security boundaries, metadata/status drift, catalog/playbook verification drift, and deep-loop executor reliability.

keyFindings.question: Q1
keyFindings.title: MCP contract drift is divergent hand-maintenance, not one broken generator
keyFindings.summary: The public tool schema, Zod validation allow-list, handler/runtime, install guide, feature catalog, and playbook carry separate contract representations and disagree about `dryRun:false`, `mode:"apply"`, and `activeOnly`.

keyFindings.question: Q3
keyFindings.title: Entity-density cache staleness has real graph-routing impact; atomic save is crash-window risk
keyFindings.summary: The entity-density cache derives graph routing signals from title/trigger phrase terms and remains stale after memory_update until invalidation/TTL/restart; atomic save indexes before pending-file promotion, creating a narrower crash window.

keyFindings.question: Q2
keyFindings.title: Metadata drift is systemic but split across multiple owners
keyFindings.summary: Graph refresh preserves active-child chronology, `deriveStatus()` can mark placeholder summaries complete, resource maps and changelog rollups are stale catalog snapshots, and description metadata can retain stale renumbering when not regenerated.

keyFindings.question: Q2
keyFindings.title: Observed metadata blast radius includes at least four false-complete 027 draft children and multiple 026 catalog surfaces
keyFindings.summary: 027 phases 003 through 006 are draft in the phase map but complete in graph metadata, while 026 has stale resource-map, changelog rollup, parent active-child, and remediation packet progress signals.

keyFindings.question: Q4
keyFindings.title: Governed-scope defects are real, but local single-user MCP calibrates them to P1
keyFindings.summary: Community fallback bypasses the scoped pipeline and causal tools operate on bare IDs, but P0 requires a shared untrusted-principal or remote/untrusted caller model.

keyFindings.question: Q5
keyFindings.title: Fan-out code can mask failed subprocesses and serialize a single multi-lineage run
keyFindings.summary: Nonzero subprocess exits are returned as ordinary worker output, the pool counts fulfilled returns as success, and `spawnSync` blocks the async worker before additional lineages can start in the same process.

keyFindings.question: Q5
keyFindings.title: Current review summaries are suspect, but per-lineage sentinels reduce evidence of missing lineages
keyFindings.summary: Eight review summaries report one lineage while ledgers record five started and five completed entries. Individual stdout files include sentinels; the suspect artifacts are summaries and one self-invocation-refused executor audit, not necessarily the review reports themselves.

keyFindings.title: Doc/schema drift is a contract-boundary problem, not one stale page
Graph metadata status drift is systemic and caused by status-source precedence
keyFindings.blastRadius: Read-only scan found 163 strong status contradictions under 026; exact count may shift with free-form status classification.

keyFindings.title: memory_update can stale graph-channel preservation until entity-density cache TTL expiry
keyFindings.blastRadius: Short entity-rich queries can over-route or under-route graph/degree until TTL expiry or an unrelated invalidation.

keyFindings.title: Atomic save commits index rows before final file promotion
keyFindings.blastRadius: Crash or promotion failure after DB commit can leave retrieval indexed to content not present at the canonical file path.

keyFindings.title: Scope/auth P0s are conditional on governed or multi-tenant isolation claims
keyFindings.blastRadius: Default local stdio: P1 correctness/security-hardening. Governed or multi-tenant mode: P0 isolation bypass.

keyFindings.title: Prior fan-out artifacts are content-usable but orchestration-suspect
keyFindings.blastRadius: Lineage reports and merged findings can inform remediation; orchestration summaries, executor provenance, concurrency claims, and iteration-cap compliance need rerun after fan-out fixes.

keyFindings.title: MCP tool contracts are split across multiple maintained sources
keyFindings.impact: Docs and tool schemas drift because no single generated contract forces parity.

keyFindings.title: Graph metadata and launch metadata require semantic freshness checks
keyFindings.impact: Resume, launch, and completion routing can trust stale pointers or placeholder-complete children.

keyFindings.title: Memory write path has real stale-routing and DB/file-ordering failure modes
keyFindings.impact: Normal operator actions can cause temporary retrieval/routing drift or indexed records pointing at non-promoted files.

keyFindings.title: Governed-scope bypasses are real, but severity depends on the declared boundary
keyFindings.impact: Community fallback can leak out-of-scope rows; causal ID tools can mutate or delete out-of-scope edges if scope is a security boundary.

keyFindings.title: Fan-out orchestration can count failed child exits as success
keyFindings.impact: Prior review/research campaign summaries may understate failed lineages and overstate concurrency.
